page: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dv3 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor, operating at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports hyper-threading, AVX, AES, BMI2, ADX, and FMA instructions, but lacks AVX-512 capabilities. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 1638 GB of bundled local storage, while containing no dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff between single-core and multi-core operations. Single-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench tests, which is also reflected in weak single-threaded text processing. However, the 64-vCPU allocation allows the server to achieve average multi-core performance across stress-ng, Geekbench, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average tier, as do database operations and Redis SET operations. Large language model inference shows average prompt processing speeds but drops to poor performance for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D64_v3 offers a high-density resource profile suitable for legacy multi-threaded workloads that do not require high single-core clock speeds. The inclusion of 1638 GB of local storage provides a qualitative advantage for workloads requiring local scratch space. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that can leverage the high core count and balanced memory capacity.
description: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dv3 family, powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4 processor with 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores. It features 256.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and 1638 GB of bundled local storage, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations achieve average ratings. The server lacks GPU acceleration and AVX-512 support. Qualitatively, it offers a dense resource profile suited for multi-threaded workloads that do not depend on high single-core speeds. It is recommended for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose VM featuring 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1638 GB local storage. It offers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core capabilities.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64_v3 is a general-purpose VM with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1638 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-core general-purpose virtual machine with bundled local storage, offering balanced multi-threaded performance for database and web serving workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon E5-2673 v4
256.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core
1638 GB of bundled local storage for data-intensive operations
Average multi-core performance contrasted with weak single-core benchmarks
Well-suited for multi-threaded software compilation, databases, and web serving
